summary: Matched the common fighter Rebound action by replacing the corresponding assembly in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with a new C translation in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Rebound.c`. The PR added the rebound header, registered the C object in `obj_files.mk`, moved the `ft_80099D9C` declaration out of the generic `ft_0877.h`, included the new header from `fighter.c`, and refined several fighter/common-data field types from unknown or integer to float based on the matched arithmetic. The generated report shows 100% matching for the new file and all listed functions.
what_changed: Removed the hand-written assembly for `ft_80099D9C`, `ftCo_ReboundStop_Anim`, the local helper at `80099E44`, `ftCo_Rebound_Anim`, `ftCo_Rebound_IASA`, `ftCo_Rebound_Phys`, and `ftCo_Rebound_Coll` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Rebound.c` implementing the rebound motion flow in C.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Rebound.h` with public declarations for the rebound entry point and callbacks.
Registered `ftCo_Rebound.c.o` in `TEXT_O_FILES` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` object.
Added a `rebound` motion-vars struct to `ftCommon_MotionVars` using `fp+2340` as `float x0` and `fp+2344` as `float anim_start`.
Changed `Fighter::dmg.x1920` from `s32` to `float`; `dmg.x191C` was already used as float in this PR.
Moved the `ft_80099D9C` declaration from `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h` to the new `ftCo_Rebound.h` header and included that header from `fighter.c`.
smart_moves: The translation introduced a small static helper, `ftCo_80099E44`, to model the shared target at address `099E44`, allowing `ftCo_ReboundStop_Anim` to remain a simple wrapper around the transition to `ftCo_MS_Rebound`.

The C implementation preserved the assembly's float data flow by typing `co_attrs.x9C`, `dmg.x1920`, and common-data fields `x3D8`/`x3DC` as floats instead of forcing casts around unknown or integer fields.
The rebound-specific use of `fp+2340` and `fp+2344` was captured in `ftCommon_MotionVars` as a named `rebound` variant, making the motion-state scratch fields more readable and reusable.
The code keeps the empty IASA callback as `void ftCo_Rebound_IASA(ftCo_GObj* gobj) {}` to match the original `blr` stub cleanly.
The `MUST_MATCH` guarded unused stack allocation documents and isolates a matching artifact rather than polluting normal builds.
The PR kept the remaining `ft_08A4.s` assembly object in the build while inserting the new C object before it, a standard incremental asm-to-C migration pattern.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter common actions, use motion-state-specific names under `ftCo_*` and add a dedicated header once a group of callbacks leaves a catch-all header.

Offsets in the `mv.co` union should be converted into action-specific structs when a matched function reveals clear semantics; here `fp+2340` and `fp+2344` became `rebound.x0` and `rebound.anim_start`.

Matched floating-point arithmetic is strong evidence for changing placeholder `UNK_T` or integer fields to `float`, especially when the assembly uses `lfs`, `fadds`, `fdivs`, `fmadds`, `fmuls`, or `stfs` at those offsets.
A `blr`-only callback can be represented as an empty function rather than leaving it in assembly.
When a decompiled function calls `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, preserving exact argument constants, float values, and motion-state IDs is central to matching.
If a function has unexplained stack behavior, a `MUST_MATCH`-guarded dummy local can be used, but it should be explicitly marked as a matching artifact.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_Rebound.c` and `ftCo_Rebound.h` follow the common fighter action file naming convention used by neighboring files such as `ftCo_Escape.c` and `ftCo_EscapeAir.c`.
Motion callbacks use the pattern `ftCo_Rebound_Anim`, `ftCo_Rebound_IASA`, `ftCo_Rebound_Phys`, and `ftCo_Rebound_Coll`.
The initial unnamed entry point remains address-style as `ft_80099D9C`, indicating the PR did not infer a higher-level descriptive name for it.
The internal shared routine is named `ftCo_80099E44`, combining the subsystem prefix with the address because stronger semantic evidence was not provided.
The header guard is address-based, `GALE01_099D9C`, matching the first function address in the file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed seven matched routines and their local sdata2 constants from `ft_08A4.s` after replacing them with C.
Used `NULL`, `0`, and `1` in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls to reproduce the original immediate/floating argument pattern.
Computed `fp->mv.co.rebound.anim_start` as `(fp->co_attrs.x9C + 0.1f) / fp->dmg.x191C`, matching the assembly's load/add/divide/store sequence.
Computed `fp->mv.co.rebound.x0` as `-fp->dmg.x1920 * (fp_x191C * p_ftCommonData->x3D8 + p_ftCommonData->x3DC)`, matching the original negate, fused multiply-add, multiply, and store pattern.
In `ftCo_Rebound_Phys`, used a float truthiness check on `rebound.x0`; if nonzero it is cleared to `0`, otherwise `ft_80084F3C` is called, reflecting the original compare-to-zero branch.
Kept `ftCo_Rebound_Coll` as a direct wrapper around `ft_80083F88` and `ftCo_Rebound_Anim` as a frames-remaining check followed by `ft_8008A2BC`.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `ft_80099D9C` or `ftCo_80099E44` with stronger rebound semantics.
Check whether `ftCommonData::x3D8` and `x3DC` receive descriptive names after more rebound or damage physics work.
Search for additional uses of `mv.co.rebound.x0` and `mv.co.rebound.anim_start` to validate or improve the field names.
Investigate whether the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack workaround was later removed or replaced by a cleaner matching pattern.
